title: Questions surrounding the taxonomic validity of the species Garlic mite-borne filamentous virus (genus Allexivirus).
author_string: Geering, Andrew D W, McTaggart, Alistair R
abstract: Garlic mite-borne filamentous virus is one of the oldest recognized allexivirus species but, paradoxically, one with the least well studied member viruses. In this paper, we review the history of this taxon and highlight problems in designating a holotype (exemplar isolate). Analyses are presented that suggest that GarMbFV is conspecific with Garlic virus A, and therefore the former taxon should be abolished.
